item.title: ABB India Ltd’s Mixed Week: -0.14% Price Change Amid Technical Shifts and Sector Volatility
item.description: ABB India Ltd closed the week ending 6 March 2026 with a marginal decline of 0.14%, settling at Rs.6,065.90, while the Sensex fell sharply by 3.00%. The stock demonstrated resilience amid a volatile market backdrop, marked by technical momentum shifts and sector-specific pressures. Despite early-week losses, ABB India staged a late recovery, outperforming the broader index and its sector on key trading days. Time: 6 hours ago

item.title: Greenlam Industries Declines 1.99% Amid Bearish Technicals and Downgrade
item.description: Greenlam Industries Ltd closed the week at Rs.234.05, down 1.99% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.238.80, while the Sensex declined 3.00% over the same period. Despite the broader market weakness, Greenlam outperformed the benchmark by 1.01 percentage points. The week was marked by a series of bearish technical developments, a downgrade to a Strong Sell rating, and persistent negative momentum, all contributing to the stock’s subdued performance. Time: 6 hours ago

item.title: RIR Power Electronics Ltd Declines 0.92% Amid Volatile Week and Market Pressure
item.description: RIR Power Electronics Ltd closed the week ending 6 March 2026 at Rs.172.60, down 0.92% from the previous Friday’s close of Rs.174.20. This modest decline came amid a turbulent week marked by sharp intraday volatility and a significant gap down on 2 March. Despite the stock’s slight fall, it outperformed the broader Sensex, which declined 3.00% over the same period, reflecting relative resilience in a challenging market environment. Time: 6 hours ago

item.title: Cholamandalam Investment Falls 6.08%: Technical Shift and Valuation Concerns Shape Week
item.description: Cholamandalam Investment & Finance Company Ltd experienced a challenging week ending 6 March 2026, with its share price declining 6.08% to close at Rs.1,625.50, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by significant intraday volatility, a downgrade in investment rating from Buy to Hold, and a notable shift in technical momentum from mildly bullish to sideways, reflecting growing caution among investors amid elevated valuation concerns and broader market pressures. Time: 6 hours ago

item.title: Ashok Leyland Ltd. Falls 7.84%: 2 Key Factors Behind the Weekly Decline
item.description: Ashok Leyland Ltd. experienced a challenging week from 2 to 6 March 2026, with its stock price declining by 7.84% to close at Rs.194.55, underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by significant intraday lows, heightened derivatives activity, and sectoral pressures that weighed on the stock’s performance despite its longer-term resilience. Time: 6 hours ago

item.title: Rane Holdings Ltd Gains 2.60%: Volatility and Technical Signals Shape the Week
item.description: Rane Holdings Ltd closed the week with a modest gain of 2.60%, outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The stock experienced significant volatility, marked by a sharp gap down at the start of the week amid market concerns, followed by a strong gap up midweek reflecting renewed positive sentiment. Despite these swings, technical indicators and a recent downgrade to a Strong Sell rating continue to weigh on the stock’s outlook. Time: 6 hours ago

item.title: RattanIndia Power Ltd Drops 3.74%: 4 Key Factors Behind the Slide
item.description: RattanIndia Power Ltd’s shares declined by 3.74% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.7.98 compared to Rs.8.29 the previous Friday. This underperformance slightly exceeded the Sensex’s 3.00% fall during the same period, reflecting persistent challenges amid weak financials, high leverage, and technical bearishness. The stock hit fresh 52-week lows twice this week, underscoring ongoing downward momentum despite intermittent intraday recoveries. Time: 6 hours ago

item.title: Vipul Ltd Falls 3.33%: 4 Key Events Shaping This Week’s Volatility
item.description: Vipul Ltd’s shares declined by 3.33% over the week ending 6 March 2026, closing at Rs.12.78 against a 3.00% fall in the Sensex. The stock experienced sharp swings, including hitting a new 52-week high early in the week before succumbing to heavy selling pressure and circuit limits. A downgrade to Strong Sell amid weak financials and mixed technicals further weighed on sentiment, resulting in a volatile trading week for the micro-cap realty stock. Time: 6 hours ago

item.title: Morarjee Textiles Ltd Falls 6.37%: 4 Key Events Shaping the Week
item.description: Morarjee Textiles Ltd experienced a turbulent week from 2 to 6 March 2026, closing down 6.37% to ₹7.05 against a 3.00% decline in the Sensex. The stock exhibited sharp intraday swings, hitting upper circuit limits on three separate days amid strong buying pressure, but also suffered a lower circuit hit on 4 March amid heavy selling. Despite these volatile moves, the company’s micro-cap status, weak fundamentals, and a Strong Sell rating from MarketsMOJO continued to weigh on investor sentiment. Time: 6 hours ago

item.title: Viceroy Hotels Ltd Gains 1.46%: 3 Key Factors Driving the Week’s Volatility
item.description: Viceroy Hotels Ltd recorded a modest weekly gain of 1.46% closing at Rs.149.30 on 6 March 2026, outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by significant intraday volatility, including a lower circuit hit on 4 March and an upper circuit trigger on 6 March, reflecting sharp swings in investor sentiment amid subdued liquidity and mixed technical signals. Time: 6 hours ago

item.title: Apollo Pipes Ltd Falls 3.03%: Mixed Technical Signals Amid Financial Struggles
item.description: Apollo Pipes Ltd experienced a turbulent week ending 6 March 2026, with its stock price declining 3.03% from Rs.359.60 to Rs.348.70, marginally underperforming the Sensex which fell 3.00% over the same period. The week was marked by a series of rating changes and shifting technical momentum, reflecting ongoing financial challenges amid mixed market signals. Despite some short-term technical improvements, the company’s fundamentals remain under pressure, contributing to the stock’s volatile performance. Time: 6 hours ago

item.title: Nibe Ordnance & Maritime Ltd Gains 10.15%: 4 Key Factors Driving the Rally
item.description: Nibe Ordnance & Maritime Ltd delivered a strong weekly performance, rising 10.15% from Rs.10.54 to Rs.11.61 between 2 and 6 March 2026, significantly outperforming the Sensex which declined 3.00% over the same period. The stock hit new 52-week and all-time highs twice during the week, reflecting robust momentum amid a challenging market backdrop marked by sector weakness and broader index volatility. Time: 6 hours ago

item.title: Hind Rectifiers Gains 2.40% Despite Market Weakness: 4 Key Factors Driving the Week
item.description: Hind Rectifiers Ltd experienced a volatile trading week from 2 to 6 March 2026, closing with a modest gain of 2.40% despite a challenging market backdrop. The stock notably outperformed the Sensex, which declined 3.00% over the same period, reflecting selective strength amid broader market weakness. Key events including a sharp gap down opening, technical downgrades, and a strong intraday surge shaped the stock’s price trajectory during the week. Time: 6 hours ago
